Title: Innovative screening for mutants affected in seed oil/protein allocation identifies TRANSPARENT TESTA7 as a regulator of oil accumulation

Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Greenhouse growth under long day conditions
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: A ugt78d1 mutation abolished the low-oil tt7 phenotype, implicating kaempferol-3-O-rhamnoside accumulation as causal. UGT78D1 expression is reported as seed-coat restricted at onset of seed development.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Reducing UGT78D1 activity in tt7-like or high-kaempferol backgrounds may restore oil content by lowering kaempferol-3-O-rhamnoside accumulation.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. UGT78D1 is a major flavonol 3-O-rhamnosyltransferase relevant to seed flavonol glycosylation. 2. ugt78d1 tt7 double mutants restored seed oil content to wild type. 3. This genetically links kaempferol-3-O-rhamnoside accumulation to oil reduction in tt7 seeds.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Seed coat at onset of seed development
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Untargeted LC-MS/MS metabolomics; mapping-by-sequencing/WGS ArrayExpress E-MTAB-14527; metabolomics MassIVE doi:10.25345/C5X05XQ87
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Genetic interaction via double mutant analysis; seed oil phenotyping by NIRS
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Seed coat at onset of seed development
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of UGT78D1 restores seed oil content in tt7 background, indicating its product negatively associates with oil accumulation
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Seed oil is a storage reserve; kaempferol glycosides affect seed coat differentiation and seed physiology
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Altering flavonol glycosylation may affect seed coat development and longevity

Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Greenhouse growth under long day conditions
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: ugt78d2 single mutants had reduced seed oil content, and ugt78d2 tt7 double mutants retained the tt7 low-oil phenotype. The paper suggests 3-O-glycosylation may switch toward 3-O-rhamnoside via UGT78D1 in ugt78d2.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Avoid UGT78D2 disruption when breeding for high seed oil, as it may exacerbate unfavorable kaempferol-rhamnoside accumulation.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. UGT78D2 contributes to flavonol 3-O-glycosylation. 2. ugt78d2 single mutants show reduced seed oil content. 3. ugt78d2 does not rescue the tt7 low-oil phenotype.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Single and double mutant analysis; seed oil phenotyping by NIRS
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Mutation reduces seed oil content and fails to suppress tt7-associated oil reduction
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Seed oil is a storage reserve; flavonol glycosides affect development/signaling
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Mutation may alter plant stature, branching, and auxin transport per cited background literature

Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Greenhouse growth under long day conditions
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: ugt89c1 single mutants had similar or slightly higher seed oil content than wild type, but ugt89c1 tt7 double mutants remained low oil, so this step is not the key causal determinant for tt7 oil reduction.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Less promising than UGT78D1 as an engineering target for rescuing oil loss in tt7-like backgrounds.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. UGT89C1 catalyzes major 7-O-rhamnosylation of flavonols. 2. Single mutants do not reduce oil content. 3. ugt89c1 mutations do not rescue the tt7 low-oil phenotype.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Later in seed development in both embryo and seed coat
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Single and double mutant analysis; seed oil phenotyping by NIRS
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Later in seed development in both embryo and seed coat
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Mutation does not suppress low seed oil in tt7 background
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Seed oil is a storage reserve; flavonol glycosides affect seed biology
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Altering seed flavonol glycosylation may affect seed coat traits

Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key branch-switch enzyme; not established as rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss of function reduces seed oil content, alters fatty acid composition, increases cell wall polysaccharides, impairs starch degradation and mucilage production, reduces seed size, and affects seed coat differentiation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: TT7 positively affects seed oil accumulation; loss of TT7 causes drastic seed oil reduction driven by accumulation of kaempferol-derived metabolites
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Mapping-by-sequencing/WGS ArrayExpress E-MTAB-14527; untargeted LC-MS/MS metabolomics MassIVE doi:10.25345/C5X05XQ87
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Seeds, mainly embryo for oil; seed coat for causal flavonol accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Mapping-by-sequencing identified a premature stop mutation in TT7 in HEM_115. 2. Independent tt7 alleles and allelism/complementation confirmed TT7 as causal for reduced seed oil. 3. tt7 mutants uniquely among tested flavonoid mutants showed strong seed oil reduction. 4. Removal of flavonoids by tt4 restored oil content, and genetic data implicated kaempferol-3-O-rhamnoside accumulation as causal.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates the B-ring 3' position of flavonoid intermediates, redirecting flux from kaempferol-type to quercetin-type flavonoids
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native 2 kb TT7 promoter in complementation construct pTT7::TT7
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Competes with and redirects flavonoid branch composition, indirectly determining accumulation of kaempferol glycosides that suppress oil biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Greenhouse growth; seed developmental stage 10 DAP to maturity for expression analyses
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mapping-by-sequencing; independent mutant analysis; allelism tests; complementation with pTT7::TT7; reciprocal crosses; double mutant analysis; RT-qPCR; NIRS and GC metabolite phenotyping; untargeted metabolomics
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: TT7 is a strategic locus linking seed coat flavonoid composition and oil accumulation. Avoid loss-of-function alleles when breeding for high seed oil; manipulate downstream kaempferol glycosylation if flavonoid traits must be modified without oil penalty.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Seed oil provides carbon and energy reserves for germination and nutritional value for food/feed

Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: First committed entry step in flavonoid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss removes flavonoids broadly; may alter seed coat traits
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: tt4 single mutants show increased seed oil content; tt4 loss rescues tt7 low-oil phenotype, showing tt7 effect is flavonoid-dependent
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. tt4 mutants had higher seed oil than wild type. 2. Double tt7 tt4 mutants restored oil content to wild-type/tt4 levels. 3. This demonstrates that tt7 low-oil phenotype depends on flavonoid accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Condenses malonyl-CoA and p-coumaroyl-CoA to form chalcone, the first dedicated flavonoid intermediate
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor pool to flavonoids whose accumulation inversely affects oil content
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Single and double mutant analysis; NIRS; RT-qPCR in tt7 tt4 background
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Supports the concept that reducing specific flavonoid flux can increase oil, but whole-pathway elimination may compromise seed coat functions; use cautiously.

Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Branch enzyme for flavonol formation; not established as rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reduced flavonol content and altered PA balance
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of FLS1 in tt7 background restores seed oil content, indicating kaempferol-derived flavonols are required for tt7 oil reduction
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. fls1 single mutants are part of the comparative metabolomic panel. 2. fls1 tt7 double mutants recovered seed oil content to wild type. 3. This supports a causal role of kaempferol-derived flavonols in tt7-mediated oil reduction.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts dihydroflavonols to flavonols such as kaempferol/quercetin aglycones
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms the flavonol pool whose kaempferol glycosides underlie the low-oil phenotype in tt7
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Double mutant analysis; NIRS; untargeted metabolomics
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Modulating FLS1 may reduce adverse kaempferol accumulation and protect oil content, but broader flavonol-dependent traits may be affected.

Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Committed toward PA branch; not established as rate-limiting for oil phenotype
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss removes PAs and alters seed coat pigmentation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: tt3 mutants have increased seed oil, but tt3 tt7 double mutants remain low oil like tt7, excluding altered PA deficiency as the main cause of tt7 oil reduction
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. tt3 single mutants show higher oil than wild type. 2. tt3 tt7 double mutants retain the low-oil tt7 phenotype. 3. Therefore altered PA composition is unlikely to be the main driver of tt7 oil reduction.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces dihydroflavonols to leucoanthocyanidins in the proanthocyanidin branch
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Competes with flavonol branch; used genetically to test whether PA branch changes explain oil phenotype
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Less direct value for oil engineering than TT7/UGT78D1 because PA branch disruption alone does not explain tt7 low-oil phenotype.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR across seed development; correlation with mutant phenotype
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. WRI1 transcript levels are significantly reduced in tt7-8 during seed development. 2. WRI1 expression is partially restored in tt7 tt4 double mutants. 3. These data suggest flavonoid-dependent repression of oil biosynthetic regulation downstream of TT7 loss.
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation of oil biosynthetic genes; inferred repression in tt7 background
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: WRI1 remains a prime engineering target to increase seed oil, but this paper suggests seed coat flavonol context can modulate its expression and should be considered in breeding stacks.
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Seed oil is a major storage and nutritional reserve
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RT-qPCR; related transcriptomic evidence discussed from prior study
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: In this paper, inferred negative input from tt7-dependent kaempferol glycoside accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Developing seeds during maturation window (10 DAP to mature seed)
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Reduced WRI1 expression is associated with reduced seed oil accumulation in tt7 mutants
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Seed development from 10 DAP to maturity
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: WRI1 is not the causal mutation of the paper but is directly assayed in Results as mechanistic downstream regulator of TT7 effect on oil accumulation.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Developing seeds from 10 DAP to maturity
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Master regulator of plant oil biosynthesis, but here acts as downstream mechanistic mediator rather than primary causal gene

Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. tt2 mutants were included among flavonoid regulatory mutants. 2. In this study, tt2 did not show a statistically significant oil difference from wild type. 3. TT2 is discussed as a PA regulator background comparator, not a specific low-oil determinant.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Limited direct support in this study for oil engineering, despite prior reports.
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: MBW-type regulatory context inferred from known pathway description
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: No significant seed oil change in this study
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as pathway comparison, but not the main mechanistic focus.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Affects PA accumulation and seed coat pigmentation
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Not decisive for the core low-oil phenotype described here

Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. tt8 mutants had significantly increased seed oil content. 2. TT8 is a PA regulator used as comparator to highlight tt7 specificity. 3. Regulatory flavonoid mutants generally increase rather than decrease oil.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential negative regulator context for oil accumulation via PA pathway; however deployment may affect seed coat traits.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Comparator showing opposite oil phenotype to tt7.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss affects PAs and seed coat properties
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Conditionally influential comparator, not the focal decisive regulator

Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ttg1 mutants had the highest increased seed oil content among tested flavonoid mutants. 2. TTG1 is a regulatory comparator supporting antagonism between parts of flavonoid pathway and oil accumulation. 3. Its phenotype contrasts sharply with tt7 low oil.
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation/scaffold function in PA gene regulation
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential route to increase oil, but likely with strong pleiotropic seed coat and epidermal effects.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Mutation strongly increases seed oil content
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Strong positive oil phenotype but used as comparator rather than mechanistic focus.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Broad epidermal and seed coat pleiotropy expected
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Strong comparator regulator but not focal decisive controller in this paper

Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. tt1 mutants were included among regulatory flavonoid mutants. 2. Mutation increased seed oil relative to wild type. 3. TT1 contributes to the contrasting pattern that most non-tt7 flavonoid regulators increase oil.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Possible comparator target to alter oil/seed coat tradeoffs, but evidence here is limited to phenotype comparison.
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Regulates PA pathway genes including BAN indirectly/positively
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May affect PA accumulation and seed coat traits

Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. tt16 mutants showed a slight reduction in seed oil content. 2. TT16 is the only other tested flavonoid-related regulator besides TT7 context showing lower oil. 3. Its effect is much weaker than tt7.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Important cautionary locus showing not all flavonoid regulators raise oil when disrupted.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Mutation slightly reduces seed oil content
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used as contrast to show complexity of flavonoid–oil interplay.
